item.title: Procter & Gamble Hygiene & Health Care Ltd Falls 3.26%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Decline
item.description: Procter & Gamble Hygiene & Health Care Ltd experienced a challenging week, with its stock price declining by 3.26% from Rs.10,876.95 to Rs.10,521.90, slightly outperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows amid persistent bearish technical signals, valuation concerns, and broader market weakness, despite reporting strong quarterly profitability. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Shipping Corporation of India Ltd’s Volatile Week: -0.73% Price Change Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: Shipping Corporation of India Ltd (SCI) closed the week ending 13 March 2026 with a slight decline of 0.73%, settling at Rs.238.90, while the Sensex fell sharply by 4.87%. The stock showed notable intraday volatility and technical momentum shifts throughout the week, reflecting a complex interplay of strong quarterly results, a downgrade to Hold, and mixed technical signals amid a broadly bearish market environment. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Va Tech Wabag Ltd Gains 0.29% Despite Market Downturn: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Va Tech Wabag Ltd closed the week marginally higher by 0.29% to Rs.1,227.50, outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s performance was marked by a volatile week featuring a sharp intraday surge on 11 March 2026, a significant gap up opening, and mixed technical signals that reflect cautious investor sentiment amid broader market weakness. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Fundviser Capital Gains 2.94%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Momentum
item.description: Fundviser Capital (India) Ltd closed the week ending 13 March 2026 at Rs.305.00, down marginally by 0.31% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.305.95. This modest decline came despite the Sensex falling sharply by 4.87% over the same period, highlighting the stock’s relative resilience amid a broadly weak market. The week was marked by multiple new 52-week and all-time highs for the stock, reflecting strong momentum within the NBFC sector and a notable divergence from the benchmark index’s downward trend. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: RSWM Ltd Declines 4.53% Amid Mixed Signals: Upgrade Meets Bearish Technicals
item.description: RSWM Ltd’s stock declined by 4.53% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.138.15 from Rs.144.70 the previous Friday. This underperformance was slightly less severe than the Sensex’s 4.87% fall, indicating a marginal relative resilience amid a broadly bearish market. The week was marked by a cautious upgrade in the company’s rating to Hold by MarketsMOJO, followed by a shift to bearish technical momentum, reflecting mixed investor sentiment and ongoing volatility. Time: 2 minutes ago

item.title: Disa India Ltd Falls 4.14%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Disa India Ltd’s shares declined by 4.14% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.11,489.95 compared to Rs.11,986.05 the previous Friday. This underperformance was slightly less severe than the Sensex’s 4.87% drop, reflecting a mixed market environment amid bearish technical signals, valuation shifts, and a notable intraday rebound midweek. The stock’s volatility and trading volumes mirrored the broader market’s cautious mood, with key events shaping investor sentiment throughout the week. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Indian Hume Pipe Gains 3.31% Amid Volatile Week: 2 Key Events Shaping the Move
item.description: Indian Hume Pipe Company Ltd recorded a modest weekly gain of 3.31% to close at Rs.327.80 on 13 Mar 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s performance was marked by a sharp rebound midweek, driven by a significant gap up and intraday surge on 11 Mar, despite a challenging broader market environment. This review analyses the key events and price movements shaping the stock’s week. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: UPL Ltd. Declines 2.98% Despite Technical Upgrade: Key Market Signals This Week
item.description: UPL Ltd. closed the week ending 13 March 2026 at ₹609.45, down 2.98% from ₹628.20 the previous Friday, yet it outperformed the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The stock experienced a volatile week marked by a technical rating upgrade to Hold, mixed momentum signals, and fluctuating price action amid broader market weakness. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Ltd Drops 10.38%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Decline
item.description: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Ltd experienced a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 10.38% to close at Rs.314.30, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% fall. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows amid persistent market weakness, sectoral headwinds, and company-specific financial pressures, despite a brief rebound midweek. This review analyses the key events and market dynamics that shaped the stock’s performance over the week. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Ipca Laboratories Gains 4.71%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Outperformance
item.description: Ipca Laboratories Ltd delivered a robust weekly performance, rising 4.71% from ₹1,490.55 to ₹1,560.80 between 9 and 13 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s resilience amid broad market weakness was underpinned by a series of valuation adjustments, technical upgrades, and strong financial results that collectively bolstered investor confidence. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Thomas Cook (India) Ltd Falls 3.74%: Key Market Moves and Technical Signals This Week
item.description: Thomas Cook (India) Ltd’s stock declined by 3.74% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.88.90 from Rs.92.35, marginally outperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% during the same period. The week was marked by a sharp drop to a 52-week low on 9 March, followed by a strong rebound on 10 March, reflecting volatile investor sentiment amid broader market weakness. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Madhucon Projects Ltd Gains 6.33%: 2 Key Events Shaping This Week’s Rally
item.description: Madhucon Projects Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 6.33%, closing at Rs.4.70 on 13 Mar 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s performance was marked by a recovery from a 52-week low and a strong buying interest that pushed it to an upper circuit, despite ongoing fundamental and technical challenges. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Tata Communications Declines 3.92%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Slide
item.description: Tata Communications Ltd experienced a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price falling 3.92% to close at ₹1,399.25, slightly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The week was marked by a technical upgrade to Hold, mixed momentum indicators, and a fresh 52-week low amid broader market weakness and sector pressures. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Optiemus Infracom Falls 11.11%: 3 Key Factors Behind the Steep Weekly Decline
item.description: Optiemus Infracom Ltd endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price falling sharply by 11.11% to close at Rs.337.80, significantly underperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The week was marked by multiple new 52-week lows, heightened volatility, and persistent financial and technical headwinds that weighed heavily on investor sentiment. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Canara Bank Falls 7.61%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Canara Bank’s shares declined by 7.61% over the week ending 13 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% in the same period. The stock faced significant price pressure amid a broadly negative market environment, marked by sharp intraday lows, elevated volatility, and sectoral weakness. Despite some technical support from its 200-day moving average, short- and medium-term indicators signalled persistent downward momentum throughout the week. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: DCW Ltd Falls 7.90% This Week: 4 Key Factors Behind the Decline
item.description: DCW Ltd’s shares declined by 7.90% over the week ending 13 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% in the same period. The stock hit fresh 52-week lows twice during the week amid sustained selling pressure, despite a brief rebound on 10 March. Weak financial results, technical bearishness, and broader market volatility combined to weigh heavily on the stock’s performance. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Bajaj Electricals Gains 4.91% Despite Market Weakness: 2 Key Events Shape the Week
item.description: Bajaj Electricals Ltd closed the week ending 13 March 2026 with a gain of 4.91%, rising from Rs.356.65 to Rs.374.15, outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low early in the week before rallying sharply midweek to reach an intraday high of Rs.408. This mixed performance was shaped by weak financial results and technical pressures, followed by a notable intraday surge amid broader market weakness. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Bajaj Finance Ltd Falls 9.99%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Bajaj Finance Ltd endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 9.99% to close at Rs.855.05, significantly underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The week was marked by persistent selling pressure, technical deterioration, and a downgrade in analyst sentiment, reflecting growing concerns amid a bearish market environment and sectoral headwinds. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Rajkamal Synthetics Ltd Falls 4.70%: Valuation Shifts and 52-Week Low Mark Key Week
item.description: Rajkamal Synthetics Ltd’s stock declined by 4.70% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.32.05 compared to Rs.33.63 the previous Friday. This performance slightly outpaced the Sensex’s 4.87% fall, reflecting a mixed but predominantly bearish sentiment amid valuation adjustments and a fresh 52-week low. Key events during the week included a valuation shift signalling improved price attractiveness and the stock hitting a new yearly low, both influencing investor sentiment and price action. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Adani Power Gains 5.62%: 5 Key Factors Driving This Week’s Momentum
item.description: Adani Power Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 5.62%, closing at Rs.146.70 on 13 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock showed resilience amid a broadly bearish market, driven by strong intraday rallies, exceptional trading volumes, and increased institutional participation. Despite a recent downgrade to a Mojo Grade of Sell, Adani Power’s technical positioning and liquidity profile have attracted significant investor interest during the week. Time: 4 minutes ago
